definition.name: commutant
definition.definition: Let π : A → B(H) be a *-representation of a unital *-algebra A on a Hilbert space H. The commutant of π(A) is the von Neumann algebra π(A)' = {T ∈ B(H) : Tπ(a) = π(a)T for all a ∈ A}. By von Neumann's bicommutant theorem (for C*-algebras), π(A)'' is the weak operator closure of π(A), and π(A)' is always a von Neumann algebra. The self-adjoint part π(A)'^{sa} = {T ∈ π(A)' : T = T*} is a real vector space whose dimension controls the "internal degrees of freedom" of the representation. For the GNS representation π_φ associated to a state φ, the commutant π_φ(A)' encodes the structure of the face generated by φ in S(A) via the Alfsen-Shultz face-commutant correspondence. Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §II.3.
